questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A state constitution requires lawmakers to review “tax expenditures,” such as exemptions, deductions, and credits, in the same budget cycle as direct spending programs. Supporters argue that reducing tax revenue has a budget effect similar to spending public money, so these policies should be examined regularly.
Which civic purpose is most directly supported by this requirement?

choices.A: Treating tax breaks as part of fiscal oversight
choices.B: Giving courts authority to collect income taxes
choices.C: Allowing agencies to spend without legal approval
choices.D: Preventing the legislature from reviewing revenue policy

questions.correctAnswerText: Treating tax breaks as part of fiscal oversight
questions.explanation: Tax exemptions, deductions, and credits reduce revenue, so reviewing them helps lawmakers evaluate fiscal effects.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A country compares two imported products after a tariff is added.
Product: Steel panels, Solar batteries
Pre-tariff price per unit: $500, $800
Tariff per unit: $75, $40
Domestic producers competing with imports: Many, Few
Which statement is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Steel panels have the higher tariff as a share of pre-tariff price.
choices.B: Solar batteries have the higher tariff as a share of pre-tariff price.
choices.C: Both products have the same tariff as a share of pre-tariff price.
choices.D: Neither product is affected by the tariff.

questions.correctAnswerText: Steel panels have the higher tariff as a share of pre-tariff price.
questions.explanation: Steel panels have a tariff of $75 on $500, or 15%; solar batteries have $40 on $800, or 5%.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Source:
In the late 1800s, some African American educators promoted industrial and agricultural training as a path to economic independence. Others argued that classical education, voting rights, and legal equality were essential for full citizenship. Both approaches responded to segregation and limited opportunity.
Which issue is most clearly reflected in the source?

choices.A: Debate over strategies for advancement under segregation
choices.B: Debate over whether the United States should purchase Alaska
choices.C: Conflict over building canals in the early republic
choices.D: Disagreement over radio entertainment in the 1920s

questions.correctAnswerText: Debate over strategies for advancement under segregation
questions.explanation: The source compares different educational and political strategies used in response to segregation.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A described map shows a mountainous country with one major north-south highway. Most factories are located in a central valley where the highway crosses two rivers. Smaller villages in high mountain areas rely on pack animals and seasonal roads.
Which geographic factor best explains why factories cluster in the central valley?

choices.A: Easier transportation and access to water routes
choices.B: Complete isolation from markets
choices.C: Greater difficulty moving goods through valleys
choices.D: Absence of rivers and roads

questions.correctAnswerText: Easier transportation and access to water routes
questions.explanation: Factories cluster where the highway crosses rivers, making movement of goods and resources easier.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A state requires independent actuarial reviews before changing public retirement benefits. The review must estimate how the change would affect future pension obligations and contributions from employees and taxpayers.
Which TWO statements best explain the civic purpose of this requirement?

choices.A: It helps officials understand long-term financial effects before changing benefits.
choices.B: It connects retirement policy to obligations paid over time.
choices.C: It gives pension funds authority to declare criminal penalties.
choices.D: It prevents taxpayers from receiving any budget information.
choices.E: It allows courts to replace the legislature in setting all wages.

questions.correctAnswerText: It helps officials understand long-term financial effects before changing benefits. | It connects retirement policy to obligations paid over time.
questions.explanation: Actuarial reviews estimate future pension obligations and contributions.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Paired sources:
Source A:
A federal official supports national rules for online privacy because companies collect data from users in many states. The official argues that one national standard would reduce confusion and protect people no matter where they live.
Source B:
A state attorney general argues that states should be allowed to create stronger privacy protections when residents face new risks. The official says state laws can respond quickly when national rules are delayed or too weak.
What is the main disagreement between the two sources?

choices.A: Whether online privacy rules should be nationally uniform or allow stronger state action
choices.B: Whether internet companies should replace state courts
choices.C: Whether states may issue passports to website users
choices.D: Whether privacy laws should apply only to foreign governments

questions.correctAnswerText: Whether online privacy rules should be nationally uniform or allow stronger state action
questions.explanation: Source A favors one national rule, while Source B supports stronger state protections.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
During the late 1800s, many Chinese immigrants worked in mining, railroad construction, agriculture, and small businesses in the West. Economic competition and racial prejudice led to violence and calls for exclusion. Federal laws later restricted immigration from China.
Which broader historical pattern is shown by this source?

choices.A: Immigration contributed to labor and community development while also provoking discrimination and exclusion
choices.B: Chinese immigrants were not involved in western economic life
choices.C: Federal immigration restrictions were unrelated to racial prejudice
choices.D: Economic competition ended all anti-immigrant sentiment

questions.correctAnswerText: Immigration contributed to labor and community development while also provoking discrimination and exclusion
questions.explanation: The source describes both immigrant labor and anti-Chinese violence and restrictions.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt: A federal court may hear a case involving citizens of different states under [D1], while state courts generally handle most disputes involving [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=diversity jurisdiction; D2=state law claims
questions.explanation: Diversity jurisdiction can allow federal courts to hear cases between citizens of different states, while state courts hear many state-law disputes.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A family has a credit card balance of $1,500. The monthly interest rate is 2%. If the family makes no payment for one month, how many dollars of interest will be added?
Type your answer as a dollar amount without the dollar sign.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A country discovers offshore natural gas reserves. The coastal region expects jobs at ports and processing facilities, while inland regions ask for a share of the revenue to fund schools and roads. National leaders debate how resource income should be distributed.
Which conclusion is best supported by this scenario?

choices.A: Natural resource wealth can create debates over regional distribution of revenue.
choices.B: Offshore resources benefit only farmers in inland regions.
choices.C: Resource income never affects public spending decisions.
choices.D: Coastal regions cannot participate in energy production.

questions.correctAnswerText: Natural resource wealth can create debates over regional distribution of revenue.
questions.explanation: Offshore gas creates jobs and revenue debates between coastal and inland regions.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A state allows tribal governments and state agencies to negotiate agreements about emergency services on roads that cross reservation boundaries. The agreements identify which responders arrive first, how costs are shared, and how communication will work.
Which civic issue is most clearly shown?

choices.A: Intergovernmental coordination across overlapping jurisdictions
choices.B: State authority to abolish tribal governments
choices.C: Private control of public emergency services
choices.D: Federal courts writing every local road policy

questions.correctAnswerText: Intergovernmental coordination across overlapping jurisdictions
questions.explanation: The agreements coordinate services where state, tribal, and local responsibilities overlap.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A table shows enrollment at historically Black colleges in one region.
Year: 1920, 1940, 1960
Enrollment: 18,000, 42,000, 96,000
Number of institutions: 24, 31, 38
Which statement is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Enrollment increased faster than the number of institutions.
choices.B: The number of institutions decreased after 1920.
choices.C: Enrollment was highest in 1920.
choices.D: Enrollment and institutions both stayed the same.

questions.correctAnswerText: Enrollment increased faster than the number of institutions.
questions.explanation: Enrollment rose from 18,000 to 96,000, while institutions rose only from 24 to 38.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A state constitution lets the legislature create regional water authorities when a watershed crosses city and county boundaries. These authorities may coordinate treatment plants, conservation rules, and drought planning, but their powers must be listed in state law.
Which statement best describes this arrangement?

choices.A: Regional public bodies may be created to manage services that cross local boundaries.
choices.B: Water authorities may operate without any legal limits.
choices.C: Counties may conduct foreign water treaties without state approval.
choices.D: Cities must stop providing all water services.

questions.correctAnswerText: Regional public bodies may be created to manage services that cross local boundaries.
questions.explanation: Regional water authorities coordinate water services across city and county boundaries under state law.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A country exports copper and imports most of its medical equipment. When copper prices rise, government revenue from mining royalties increases. At the same time, a weaker national currency makes imported medical equipment more expensive.
Which conclusion is best supported by this scenario?

choices.A: A country can benefit from one global price change while being hurt by another trade-related cost.
choices.B: Export prices and exchange rates never affect public budgets.
choices.C: Imported medical equipment becomes cheaper when currency weakens.
choices.D: Mining royalties have no connection to government revenue.

questions.correctAnswerText: A country can benefit from one global price change while being hurt by another trade-related cost.
questions.explanation: Higher copper prices increase royalty revenue, but a weaker currency raises import costs.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A state constitution says that counties may not create criminal penalties unless the legislature has clearly given them that authority. Counties may pass local rules about certain services, but punishment for crimes must be authorized by state law.
Which civic principle is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Local governments have only the legal powers granted to them
choices.B: Counties may create any criminal law they choose
choices.C: Criminal law is written only by private businesses
choices.D: State legislatures cannot limit county authority

questions.correctAnswerText: Local governments have only the legal powers granted to them
questions.explanation: Counties may create penalties only when state law grants that authority.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
During the 1970s, disability rights activists organized sit-ins, lawsuits, and public campaigns to demand access to schools, transportation, workplaces, and public buildings. Activists argued that physical barriers and discriminatory policies excluded people from full participation.
Which TWO conclusions are best supported by this description?

choices.A: Disability rights activism connected civil rights claims to access in everyday public life.
choices.B: Activists used legal and public-pressure strategies to seek policy change.
choices.C: Disability rights activism focused only on foreign military policy.
choices.D: Physical access had no connection to participation in society.
choices.E: Public transportation was never part of disability rights concerns.

questions.correctAnswerText: Disability rights activism connected civil rights claims to access in everyday public life. | Activists used legal and public-pressure strategies to seek policy change.
questions.explanation: Sit-ins, lawsuits, and campaigns targeted barriers in schools, transit, work, and public buildings.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A region compares land and population.
Area: River plain, Hill country, Coastal strip
Land area: 40,000 sq mi, 70,000 sq mi, 10,000 sq mi
Population: 8 million, 3.5 million, 5 million
Which area has the highest population density?

choices.A: River plain
choices.B: Hill country
choices.C: Coastal strip
choices.D: River plain and hill country equally

questions.correctAnswerText: Coastal strip
questions.explanation: The coastal strip has 5 million people over 10,000 square miles, or 500 people per square mile, the highest density.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A state requires large public contracts to include performance benchmarks, such as completion dates and service-quality measures. If the contractor misses benchmarks without a valid reason, the state may reduce payments.
Which conclusion is best supported by this policy?

choices.A: Contract rules can link public payments to measurable results.
choices.B: Public contracts may never include performance standards.
choices.C: Contractors may decide how much public money they receive.
choices.D: Governments cannot purchase services from private companies.

questions.correctAnswerText: Contract rules can link public payments to measurable results.
questions.explanation: Benchmarks connect contractor payments to completion dates and service quality.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A city compares two options for financing a water project.
Option: 10-year bond, 20-year bond
Annual payment: $4.4 million, $2.8 million
Total interest paid: $6 million, $16 million
Which statement is best supported by the table?

choices.A: The 20-year bond has lower annual payments but higher total interest.
choices.B: The 10-year bond has higher total interest than the 20-year bond.
choices.C: Both bonds have the same annual payment.
choices.D: The 20-year bond has no interest cost.

questions.correctAnswerText: The 20-year bond has lower annual payments but higher total interest.
questions.explanation: The 20-year bond has a lower annual payment, $2.8 million, but higher total interest, $16 million.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt: When a state constitution allows citizens to sue the state only under conditions set by law, it reflects [D1]. When the state consents to be sued for certain claims, it creates [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=sovereign immunity; D2=a limited waiver
questions.explanation: Sovereign immunity limits lawsuits against the state unless the state consents through a waiver.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A public health program has three funding sources.
Source: Federal funds, State funds, County funds
Amount: $18 million, $12 million, $10 million
What percent of the program’s funding comes from federal funds?
questions.explanation: Total funding is $40 million; $18 million from federal funds equals 45%.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A state requires counties to provide certain public health services, but it allows counties to choose whether to operate clinics directly, contract with nonprofit providers, or share services with neighboring counties.
Which statement best describes this state-local relationship?

choices.A: The state sets required outcomes while counties choose among approved methods.
choices.B: Counties may ignore all state public health requirements.
choices.C: Nonprofit providers become state legislatures.
choices.D: Public health services cannot be delivered locally.

questions.correctAnswerText: The state sets required outcomes while counties choose among approved methods.
questions.explanation: Counties must provide services but may choose direct operation, contracts, or shared services.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A coastal shipping route takes 9 days. A longer route around a conflict zone takes 14 days. How many additional days does the longer route require?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: 14 days minus 9 days equals 5 additional days.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Source:
In the 1980s and 1990s, hip-hop music, dance, fashion, and visual art spread from local urban neighborhoods to national and global audiences. Artists often addressed community life, economic inequality, policing, and identity.
Which broader historical pattern is shown by the source?

choices.A: Local cultural expression can spread widely while carrying social commentary.
choices.B: Hip-hop remained limited to one neighborhood and had no social themes.
choices.C: Global audiences prevented American cultural forms from spreading.
choices.D: Music and art cannot reflect social conditions.

questions.correctAnswerText: Local cultural expression can spread widely while carrying social commentary.
questions.explanation: Hip-hop spread beyond local neighborhoods and often addressed social issues.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

targets.T1: A public body created to coordinate services across local boundaries
targets.T2: A measurable requirement used to evaluate contract performance
targets.T3: A rule affecting when a government may be sued

questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Regional authority; T2=Performance benchmark; T3=Sovereign immunity
questions.explanation: A regional authority coordinates services, a benchmark measures performance, and sovereign immunity affects lawsuits against government.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A producer signs contracts to buy wheat from three countries instead of relying on one supplier. The company says the strategy may cost slightly more but reduces the risk that drought or political conflict in one country will stop production.
Which economic idea is most clearly shown?

choices.A: Diversifying suppliers can reduce supply-chain risk.
choices.B: Buying from more suppliers always eliminates all costs.
choices.C: International sourcing prevents businesses from producing goods.
choices.D: Political conflict never affects supply chains.

questions.correctAnswerText: Diversifying suppliers can reduce supply-chain risk.
questions.explanation: Buying from multiple countries reduces reliance on one supplier that could be disrupted.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A state constitution creates a public lands trust. Revenue from leasing certain public lands must support public schools, and officials must manage the lands for long-term benefit rather than short-term private gain.
Which civic purpose is most directly supported by this rule?

choices.A: Managing public assets for a designated public purpose
choices.B: Allowing officials to transfer public lands for personal benefit
choices.C: Replacing school funding with private gifts only
choices.D: Preventing the state from managing public land

questions.correctAnswerText: Managing public assets for a designated public purpose
questions.explanation: The lands trust dedicates lease revenue to public schools and long-term benefit.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A court reviews a city fee charged to new developments for road improvements. The court says the fee is valid only if it is reasonably related to the traffic impact caused by the new development.
Which conclusion is best supported by the ruling?

choices.A: Local fees on development should be connected to the public costs created by that development.
choices.B: Cities may charge any fee without a connection to public costs.
choices.C: Road improvements cannot be funded by local governments.
choices.D: Courts may not review development fees under any standard.

questions.correctAnswerText: Local fees on development should be connected to the public costs created by that development.
questions.explanation: The fee must be reasonably related to traffic impacts from new development.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt: During the late twentieth century, many older industrial cities invested in museums, universities, medical centers, and waterfront redevelopment. These efforts reflected a shift toward [D1] after losses in [D2].
questions.correct: D1=service- and knowledge-based economies; D2=traditional manufacturing employment
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=service- and knowledge-based economies; D2=traditional manufacturing employment
questions.explanation: Redevelopment around universities, medicine, and museums reflects a shift after manufacturing losses.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A county transportation fund receives $48 million. It spends $20 million on bridges, $14 million on bus routes, and $6 million on bike lanes.
How much money remains in the fund?

choices.A: $6 million
choices.B: $8 million
choices.C: $14 million
choices.D: $40 million

questions.correctAnswerText: $8 million
questions.explanation: $20 million + $14 million + $6 million = $40 million spent; $48 million minus $40 million leaves $8 million.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A state allows local governments to form joint purchasing cooperatives. Through a cooperative, several towns can buy road salt, emergency equipment, or software together at lower prices than each town could get alone.
Which civic purpose is most directly supported by this arrangement?

choices.A: Helping local governments reduce costs by cooperating on purchases
choices.B: Allowing towns to conduct foreign trade policy
choices.C: Replacing local governments with private stores
choices.D: Preventing towns from buying public supplies

questions.correctAnswerText: Helping local governments reduce costs by cooperating on purchases
questions.explanation: Joint purchasing lets towns combine buying power for lower prices.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A state compares road funding needs.
Region: Mountain, Plains, Metro
Lane miles: 8,000, 12,000, 5,000
Average repair cost per lane mile: $90,000, $45,000, $120,000
Which TWO statements are supported by the data?

choices.A: Metro has the highest repair cost per lane mile.
choices.B: Plains has the greatest number of lane miles.
choices.C: Mountain has the lowest repair cost per lane mile.
choices.D: Metro has the greatest number of lane miles.
choices.E: Plains and Metro have the same repair cost per lane mile.

questions.correctAnswerText: Metro has the highest repair cost per lane mile. | Plains has the greatest number of lane miles.
questions.explanation: Metro has the highest repair cost at $120,000 per lane mile, and Plains has the most lane miles at 12,000.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A described map shows three ports connected by sea lanes to factories in several countries. One port specializes in container cargo, another in oil tankers, and the third in refrigerated food shipments. Nearby cities have grown around each port’s specialized services.
Which conclusion is best supported by the described map?

choices.A: Port specialization can shape nearby urban economies.
choices.B: All ports perform exactly the same economic role.
choices.C: Sea lanes prevent cities from developing.
choices.D: Refrigerated shipping has no relationship to food trade.

questions.correctAnswerText: Port specialization can shape nearby urban economies.
questions.explanation: Different port functions support different nearby services and urban economic activities.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A table shows changes in U.S. household spending patterns.
Year: 1950, 1980, 2010
Food at home: 22%, 14%, 8%
Health care: 4%, 7%, 16%
Which statement is best supported by the table?

choices.A: The share spent on food at home decreased while the share spent on health care increased.
choices.B: Spending shares for food at home and health care both decreased.
choices.C: Health care had the higher share in 1950.
choices.D: Food at home had the same share in all three years.

questions.correctAnswerText: The share spent on food at home decreased while the share spent on health care increased.
questions.explanation: Food at home fell from 22% to 8%, while health care rose from 4% to 16%.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A state constitution requires public universities to follow open admissions standards for in-state students who complete certain high school courses and meet a minimum grade requirement. Universities may set additional standards for selective programs, but they must apply those standards consistently.
Which claim is best supported by the source?

choices.A: Public education access rules can combine broad eligibility with consistent program standards.
choices.B: Public universities may change standards differently for each applicant without rules.
choices.C: State constitutions cannot address public education.
choices.D: Selective programs must admit every applicant automatically.

questions.correctAnswerText: Public education access rules can combine broad eligibility with consistent program standards.
questions.explanation: The source combines open admissions rules with consistently applied standards for selective programs.
